name: Take Charge Credit Consulting
description_short: Seattle-based non-profit credit consulting organization offering personal credit repair, business credit building, and trauma-informed financial education with a mission to reduce the racial wealth gap.
description_long:
Take Charge Credit Consulting is a national organization headquartered in Seattle, Washington that focuses on credit improvement and financial empowerment for underserved communities. Founded with an explicit mission to reduce the racial wealth gap for minorities and underserved populations, the company addresses both the structural causes of wealth disparities and practical solutions through credit access.

The company offers three primary service tiers: personal credit improvement (credit repair programs, annual credit checks, and rebuilding plans), business credit building services, and entrepreneurship consulting. They also provide trauma-informed financial educational workshops and outreach consulting for organizations. Clients receive monthly consulting calls, personalized game plans, and educational content alongside their core services. A free credit gameplan is available as an entry point.

What distinguishes Take Charge is their explicit social mission and community-oriented approach. Rather than operating as a traditional for-profit credit repair company, they frame credit improvement as a tool for reducing systemic wealth gaps, backed by education about the racial credit gap (average Black credit scores are 57 points lower, costing approximately $76,000 more in home-buying interest). They offer a Community Sponsor Program allowing individuals to pay for underserved community members' credit programs. The founder, Tierra Bonds, is highlighted in testimonials for transparency about fees and personalized attention.

The primary limitation is that detailed pricing information is not published on the website—clients must book a consultation to understand costs. While the social mission is compelling, the website does not provide specific data on success rates, average credit score improvements, or typical program timelines. The services appear positioned between non-profit credit counseling and commercial credit repair, though the mission-driven model suggests closer alignment with community-based assistance.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Explicit social mission focused on reducing the racial wealth gap, not profit maximization
Transparency about fees and program details highlighted in customer testimonials
Offers free credit gameplan entry point with no upfront commitment required
Provides monthly consulting calls included in credit repair programs
Delivers trauma-informed financial education workshops for organizations and communities
Community Sponsor Program allows supporters to fund services for underserved individuals
Founder Tierra Bonds personally handles consultations and is knowledgeable per testimonials
cons: Pricing and service costs not published on website; requires consultation to learn prices
No success rate data, average credit score improvements, or program timelines disclosed
Limited online presence for a national organization—minimal social media or third-party reviews visible
Testimonials section shows only one named review (Shim Namala), limiting transparency on typical results
Business credit and entrepreneurship services mentioned but not detailed on services page
best_for: Individuals from underserved or minority communities seeking culturally-aligned credit counseling
Mission-driven consumers who prioritize supporting organizations focused on reducing wealth gaps
Business owners and entrepreneurs needing credit building and financial coaching together
Organizations looking for trauma-informed financial education workshops and community outreach
diagnosis: Take Charge Credit Consulting is best for individuals from underserved communities seeking credit improvement aligned with a social justice mission, and for organizations wanting community-based financial education. The main caveat is that pricing is non-transparent and requires a consultation, and success metrics are not publicly disclosed, making it difficult to assess typical outcomes before committing.
services: Credit Repair & Rebuilding Programs with personalized rebuilding plans
Personal Credit Consulting with monthly 1-on-1 calls
Annual Credit Check and credit profile analysis
Educational content and resources on credit, business, and entrepreneurship
Personalized game plans for credit improvement
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
